name: Discrete System Specification
description: Definition of a Wymorian discrete system as a quintuple with associated properties, constraints, and classification rules.

elements.text: Z is the name of the system.
SZ is the set of states of the discrete system Z.
SZ is a set not empty.
IZ is the set of inputs of the discrete system Z.
OZ is the set of outputs of the discrete system Z.
NZ is the next state function of the discrete system Z.
NZ ∈ FNS(SZ × IZ, SZ) if IZ is not empty; NZ ∈ FNS(SZ, SZ) if IZ is empty.
RZ is the readout function of the discrete system Z.
RZ ∈ FNS(SZ, OZ) if OZ is not empty; RZ = ∅ if OZ is empty.
If both IZ and OZ are empty, the system Z is said to be closed.
If neither IZ nor OZ are empty, the system Z is said to be open.
The time scale of the discrete system Z is denoted TZ and is defined as follows: TZ = IJS++.
